regressions.reasoning: Stage 06 explicitly tasks the agent with fixing TLS on demo.example.com, the same hostname stage 01's oracle tests. Once the agent configures TLS on that Ingress, nginx-ingress defaults to ssl-redirect=true, so HTTP requests to demo.example.com now receive a 308 redirect to HTTPS — exactly what the oracle sees. The HTTP-only check in stage 01's oracle is stale because stage 06 legitimately changed that domain to require HTTPS.

regressions.reasoning: Stage_03 configured the canary Ingress to route on header 'X-Canary: beta', but stages 05, 07, and 10 each legitimately reconfigure that same canary Ingress resource to route on different headers ('X-Feature: v2', 'X-Canary: always'). Any of those later stages would overwrite stage_03's annotation, causing stage_03's oracle to find 'stable' on a request that no longer carries a matching canary header value. The stale re-check no longer applies.

regressions.reasoning: Stage 07 (and stage 10) assign the same canary Ingress resource to use 'X-Canary: always' as the routing header, which overwrites stage 05's 'X-Feature: v2' annotation — so the regression sweep correctly sees that 'X-Feature: v2' no longer routes to canary, but this is an expected consequence of a later stage legitimately reconfiguring the same Ingress object, not agent carelessness.
